top_stories.headline: Eleven journalists arrested in two-day operation while covering protest in Tallahassee
top_stories.weather_note: pushes PRESS higher — currently elevated (14 this week vs. 8 90-day average).

top_stories.headline: 5th Circuit upholds voter ID requirements 3-2; civil-rights coalitions file emergency appeal
top_stories.weather_note: keeps COURTS steady — currently unsettled (23 this week vs. 22 90-day average).

top_stories.headline: DOJ rejects 14 of 18 recent FOIA requests citing 'national security'
top_stories.weather_note: pushes SUNLIGHT higher — currently opaque (61% denial rate vs. 54% 90-day average).

top_stories.headline: Estimated 30,000 march in Twin Cities for voting rights; police report 4 use-of-force incidents nationally
top_stories.weather_note: pushes STREETS higher — currently active (147 this week vs. 125 90-day average).

top_stories.headline: 26 new federal rules and 3 executive actions issued this week; HHS data-sharing comment window closes Friday
top_stories.weather_note: pushes RECORD higher — currently accelerating (38 this week vs. 27 90-day average).

indicators.plain_count: 14 reporters had trouble doing their job this week
indicators.plain_count_kid: 14 reporters got hurt or stopped this week
indicators.plain_subtitle: arrests · equipment seized · denials of access
indicators.plain_subtitle_kid: got arrested, lost cameras, or weren't let in
indicators.what_it_is: Press freedom incidents in the United States — when reporters are arrested, assaulted, or stopped from covering the news.
indicators.why_it_matters: A free press is how we know what's happening. When reporters can't work, we can't know.

events.text: Two journalists detained while covering protest in Atlanta, GA.
Equipment seized from photographer at federal courthouse, Tampa.
Press credentials denied at executive briefing for nine outlets.
Eleven reporters arrested in single overnight action, Tallahassee.

indicators.plain_count_kid: judges made 23 big decisions this week
indicators.plain_subtitle_kid: about voting, speaking up, and the president's power
indicators.plain_subtitle_es: votación · libertad de expresión · poder ejecutivo
indicators.what_it_is: Federal court rulings that change what's legal — voting access, free speech, executive power, civil rights.
indicators.why_it_matters: Courts decide what the rules of the country are right now. Lots of rulings in one week means lots of changes at once.

events.text: 5th Circuit upholds voter ID requirements (3-2). Civil-rights coalitions appealing.
9th Circuit blocks executive order on asylum processing pending review.
D.C. District grants press access to immigration hearings (1st Amendment).

indicators.plain_count: 61% of public records requests denied or stalled
indicators.plain_count_kid: the government hid 6 out of every 10 things people asked about
indicators.plain_count_es: 61% de las solicitudes públicas denegadas o estancadas
indicators.plain_subtitle_kid: people asked, and most didn't get an answer
indicators.plain_subtitle_es: FOIA · ventana de 30 días · agencias federales
indicators.what_it_is: The percent of Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) requests that the federal government denies, redirects, or sits on past the legal deadline.
indicators.why_it_matters: When the government won't say what it's doing, you can't tell whether it's working for you. Higher percent = less you can know.

events.text: DOJ rejected 14 of 18 FOIA requests filed in past 30 days as 'national security'.
EPA backlog now exceeds statutory 20-day window on 213 active requests.
DHS redirected 9 inquiries to ICE FOIA office; ICE has 14-month median response.

indicators.plain_count: 4 days in session, 42 votes (28 party-line, 14 bipartisan)
indicators.plain_count_kid: Congress worked 4 days. They voted 42 times — only 14 with both teams agreeing.
indicators.plain_count_es: 4 días en sesión, 42 votaciones (28 de partido, 14 bipartidistas)
indicators.plain_subtitle_kid: the people who make laws · this week
indicators.what_it_is: Whether Congress is actually showing up and deliberating — days in session, votes cast, and how much voting crosses party lines.
indicators.why_it_matters: Congress writes the laws. If they're not showing up, voting, or working across parties, the system is stuck.

events.text: 42 roll-call votes this week. 28 along party lines, 14 bipartisan.
Senate cloture motion on judicial nominees: 51-48.

indicators.plain_count_kid: 147 protests in 41 cities · 4 times police used force
indicators.plain_count_es: 147 protestas en 41 ciudades · 4 incidentes con uso de la fuerza
indicators.plain_subtitle_kid: people gathering to speak up about something
indicators.what_it_is: Protests, rallies, and demonstrations across the country — and any time police use force to respond.
indicators.why_it_matters: When lots of people are in the streets, they're trying to tell the system something. The map shows where the loudest voices are right now.

events.text: Protests in 41 cities. Largest gathered ~30,000 in Minneapolis-St. Paul.
Use-of-force incidents reported in 4 cities (Tampa, Phoenix, Portland, NYC).
Largest single-day mobilization since previous summer.

indicators.plain_count: 38 federal actions this week (26 rules, 9 comment windows, 3 executive orders)
indicators.plain_count_kid: the president and agencies made 38 new rules and orders this week
indicators.plain_count_es: 38 acciones federales esta semana (26 reglas, 9 períodos de comentario, 3 órdenes ejecutivas)
indicators.plain_subtitle_kid: new rules from the president and his offices
indicators.what_it_is: How many new rules, executive orders, and proclamations the executive branch published this week, compared to its 90-day average pace.
indicators.why_it_matters: This is the speed of change in how the government runs. Faster doesn't always mean bad — but a flood means a lot is shifting at once.

events.text: 26 new rules issued. 9 comment periods opened. 3 executive actions.
Notable: HHS rule on data-sharing comment window closes Friday.
